title: Enaleh

{{Quote|Variously known as 'flying eels' or 'air snakes,' helas are long, serpentine amphibians that use a biologically generated repulsorlift field to 'swim' through the moist atmosphere of Enaleh.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
'''Enaleh''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]. At some point between [[12,000 BBY/Legends|12,000 BBY]] and [[11,000 BBY]], Enaleh became a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]] [[Colonist/Legends|outpost]] and was known by then as '''Ord Enaleh'''. The planet had a [[Water/Legends|moist]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and its surface features included [[Grass/Legends|grasslands]], freshwater [[Ocean/Legends|seas]], and lakes.

Two [[Species/Legends|species]] of creatures [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to Enaleh were the [[slard]]s, [[Feline/Legends|feline]] [[Diet/Legends|predators]] that leaped high into the air while [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]]; and the [[hela]]s, [[Snake/Legends|serpentine]] [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibians]] that exhibited schooling behavior and floated through Enaleh's atmosphere. Helas could also sense mechanically [[Repulsorlift generator/Legends|generated]] [[repulsorlift field]]s and were drawn to their sources.

==Description==
{{Quote|The main predators of the helas are the slards, large, quadrupedal felines that live in the Enaleh grasslands.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Enaleh was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Creatures" /> located in the [[Enaleh system/Legends|Enaleh system]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Southern Core]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planet had a [[Water/Legends|moist]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], and among its surface features were [[Grass/Legends|grasslands]] occasionally interrupted by groups of [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. Enaleh's [[Hydrosphere/Legends|hydrosphere]] included freshwater [[Ocean/Legends|seas]] and lakes. The bottom of the those water reservoirs had deposits of silt rich with [[Carbon/Legends|carbonic]] compounds.<ref name="Creatures" />

Enaleh's [[Species/Legends|fauna]] included the [[slard]]s, the [[hela]]s, and tiny flying [[Insect/Legends|insects]]. The slards were large, [[Feline/Legends|feline]] [[Diet/Legends|predators]] that lived in the planet's grasslands. A slard [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] by hiding in the grasses, then leaping up high in the air and capturing a hela as it was flying above it.<ref name="Creatures" />

===Helas===
{{Quote|Like many aquatic animals, helas are filter feeders. However, instead of swimming through liquid, the helas fly through the moist atmosphere of Enaleh, their mouths open wide, capturing thousands of tiny, flying insects every hour.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Helas were a species of two-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long [[Snake/Legends|serpentine]] [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibians]] that used a biologically generated [[repulsorlift field]] to float through Enaleh's atmosphere. The creatures always organized themselves in schools of approximately one hundred individuals and spent their entire lives flying over Enaleh's grasslands. Being filter feeders, helas captured the planet's flying insects in their open [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]].<ref name="Creatures" />

During the helas' [[Standard year/Legends|yearly]] [[Reproduction/Legends|breeding]] period, the amphibians gathered above Enaleh's seas and lakes. At first, the [[Gender/Legends|female]] helas deposited large numbers of [[Egg/Legends|eggs]] on the surface of a lake. Subsequently, the males dove repeatedly into the water, releasing gametes that interacted with the carbon compounds in the lake's silt. That reaction caused the release of large amounts of [[Carbon dioxide/Legends|carbon dioxide]] into the water, facilitating the fertilization of the eggs and promoting the growth of [[Plant/Legends|plants]] in the lake, which served as [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] for [[embryo]]nic helas.<ref name="Creatures" />

Helas were notable for their unusual behavior in the presence of mechanical [[Repulsorlift generator/Legends|repulsorlift generators]]. The creatures were able to perceive artificial [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] fields over long distances and were drawn to their sources. Upon encountering the source generating the field, helas either attacked the device or adopted it as a member of their school, floating through the air alongside it and matching its movements.<ref name="Creatures" />

Occasionally, helas were exported offworld and kept in [[Aeroquarium|aeroquaria]]; however, those specimens had a short lifespan and rarely bred. Overall, the creatures were a rare sight on other planets.<ref name="Creatures" />

==History==
[[File:Eleventh_Pius_Dea_Crusade.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Ord Enaleh was a Galactic Republic outpost during the Pius Dea Era.]]
Enaleh served as an [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]] [[Colonist/Legends|outpost]] for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Pius Dea Era]], a period of [[Time/Legends|time]] that lasted between [[12,000 BBY/Legends|12,000 BBY]] and [[11,000 BBY]]. During that era, the planet was known as Ord Enaleh.<ref name="EGTW" /> Enaleh was mentioned during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in an entry of a [[Holo-brochure|holobrochure]] for the [[Tagge Holographic Museum]] of [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] dedicated to the hela.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|Many scientists have attempted to locate and examine the organ which allows the helas to generate their repulsorlift field, but none have been successful. However, a number of smugglers have found that they can supplement their incomes by providing helas to any of a number of research institutions.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Several [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] captured and brought helas to a number of research institutions in return for payment. Many [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] were interested in locating and examining the creatures' organ responsible for the generation of the repulsorlift field.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Behind the scenes==
Enaleh was first mentioned in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The section of the book mentioning the planet was authored by [[Chuck Truett]] and illustrated by [[David Plunkett]].<ref name="Creatures" />

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Enaleh [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Enaleh, in grid square L-13,<ref name="TEA" /> while the [[2012]] book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' provided the alternate designation of "Ord Enaleh" for the planet.<ref name="EGTW" />
